head: d8dc7e845d3a7618fdf41525b67b8bac83d05d28
scope: Public concepts and material workarounds introduced or materially changed by PR 346

disposition_rule.keep: The concept and its public placement fit the confirmed activation-context frame.
disposition_rule.reshape: The behavior matters, but its public shape, dependency graph, or lifecycle owner conflicts with the frame.
disposition_rule.remove: The item exists only to work around the current shape or duplicates a Lite responsibility.
disposition_rule.missing: The confirmed frame requires proof that the current PR does not provide.

public_concepts.reason: The namespaces group one meaning and keep the root entry small.
These remain declared Lite graph edges and are outside the session activation correction.
One neutral streaming port plus a scalar adapter preserves provider choice without a second agent loop.

Readiness, identity, schema, the inert effect flow, and eager activation of every declared role tool are intended strengths. The helper adds framework dependencies to the returned resource's static graph; runtime-provided tags and missing-tag failure are accepted.
Lazy skill content is a declared resource and its session dependency is present in the returned static graph. Runtime-provided tags and eager resolution through the role remain accepted.
Roles are resources whose complete declared tool and skill tree resolves before the model call. Authority filtering is encoded in the returned resource graph and does not hide a physical effect edge.

Keep a streaming flow for the model and dispatch loop, including its required and optional runtime tags. DKR-1 and DKR-2 must only reshape the activation binding and generic cancellation source; missing runtime tags remain an accepted runtime failure.
The definition declares its role, turn, and child session run as inert graph data. It preserves child-work admission without a context-bound method or an undeclared effect.
Canonical authority, narrowing, and stable fingerprints are independent of activation ownership and are already tested at the scope seam.
Plain durable data can outlive activations and supports checkpoint, resume, evidence, memory, and schedules.

Keep activation-local semantic registries and durable record mutation. Move generic child cancellation, joining, and activation shutdown to ExecutionContext timing, and make ordinary close distinct from durable finish.
Keep per-exec bindings, but group activation configuration under one clear namespace so a channel can bind a session at controller.exec({ tags }).

The runtime-provided facts and missing-tag behavior are accepted. DKR-1 and DKR-3 should test whether the public grouping and repeated propagation can become more ergonomic while keeping all required tags visible to the static graph and extensions.
Namespaced tags carrying flows make physical effects explicit, replaceable, and authority-checkable.
A flow that reloads durable state, verifies authority, and returns tags is a scope-native resume seam.

Keep work admission before turn execution and streaming settlement. Remove the nested { work, input } ceremony and manual signal/current-tag bridge if per-exec tags and context timing can carry the activation.
Keep finish as the only fence, join, commit, and seal business flow. Remove its use from ordinary context cleanup.
Persisted waiting work and authoritative wake are required for sessions that outlive one activation.
Keep child branches, fail-fast policy, and versioned merge. Recheck whether execution joining belongs in context timing while semantic work settlement and merge remain session concerns.
Keep attempt and epoch fencing plus durable domain events. Add a safe context observation model for extensions instead of treating SessionEvent as the whole trace model.
Configurable Standard Schema validation supports Zod and Valibot without a built-in engine.

Policy, session authority, and physical implementors are all declared dependencies of named effect flows. Runtime-provided tags may fail at execution, and policy still fails closed before the physical edge.

Keep module-level attempts, scalar compatibility, and scope-owned physical adapters. Replace the SDK abortSignal tag bridge and align continuation ownership with the activation context without losing provider cancellation isolation.
Keep public fakes and scope-owned setup, but make the common helper prove multiple session activations in one server scope without mandatory pre-resolution.

material_workarounds.reason: The public docs require an ordering call that users can omit, producing a temporary runtime whose changes disappear.
Ordinary resource cleanup changes live and record status to finishing even though the docs say cleanup only releases owned resources.
Targeted work cancellation is semantic session behavior. Parent-child cancellation and joining duplicate ExecutionContext timing and should be proven in Lite.
The tag manually carries generic execution cancellation through session, turn, provider, and sandbox code. DKR-2 must test a context-native signal before removal.

Tool and skill helpers reserve framework dependency names, then place those dependencies in the returned resource's static graph. This prevents an author from replacing required session semantics inside a definition; it is not a hidden effect edge.
run and boundary flows repeatedly construct the same session, work, branch, authority, attempt, epoch, and signal tag set. The facts remain important, but the fanout is not the public model.
Every channel must map domain input into { work, input } even though controller.exec already supports per-execution tags.
One SDK test proves role isolation with two independent scopes. The server requirement is multiple sibling session contexts in one scope; the bash adapter test is the stronger existing seed.

missing_proofs.reason: Expected-version commit detects a later conflict, but the public session surface has no live activation acquisition or release edge before effects.
The SDK currently compensates with Runtime AbortControllers. The generic parent cancel-and-join rule is not proven by this PR.
SessionEvent records domain activity, but no public safe projection contract proves that extensions observe channel, session, work, turn, and tool context without sensitive tags.
The PR has channel and schedule adapters but no durable issue queue, worker concurrency, acknowledge, reject, or graceful watcher proof.
The database example proves read-only analysis and host-side memory acceptance, not evidence freshness, independent hypothesis verification, or authorized idempotent GitHub publication.

already_proven.claim: Canonical subpaths pack and keep their types off the root entry.
Tool readiness can fail before model or database effects.
Read-only parallel analysis, join-before-merge, and host-side acceptance have an executable example test.
Two session contexts in one scope can isolate sandbox resources and cancellation.
Waiting state can be checkpointed, loaded, rebound, and woken.
Provider attempt adapters preserve scalar compatibility and provider-specific streaming.

frame_clarifications: Activating the complete statically declared dependency tree is intended because tests replace its leaves through the scope seam without mocking the tree.
Required tags are runtime-provided. Missing-tag runtime failure is accepted in this run.
Static analysis for missing runtime tags is deferred.
A framework helper may add declared resource dependencies. A physical effect that is absent from the graph remains forbidden.
must_not_be_lost: One static role-tool-skill-subagent declaration before a model call.
Tool readiness and schema identity before advertisement, with validation repeated at dispatch.
Authority narrowing, attempt and epoch fencing, and fail-closed sandbox policy before physical effects.
Plain durable session, work, attempt, branch, invocation, artifact, memory, schedule, and continuation records.
Explicit checkpoint and finish flows; cleanup must not commit, schedule, publish, accept memory, or call a model.
Wait, reload, authority rebind, wake, retry, fork, join, merge, steering, and ordered domain-event semantics.
Provider scalar compatibility, streaming events, continuation isolation, consumer-stop cleanup, and scope-owned physical adapters.
The scope as the only test seam, with no shared scope factory or module mocks.
No built-in tools, automatic tool collection, MCP expansion, or model-authorized publication in this frame.

steering_decision.summary: Continue PR 346, but reshape its activation and lifecycle spine before adding the issue-triage slice.
steering_decision.dkr_1_input: Test whether controller(flow).exec({ input, tags }) can own one complete activation without pre-resolution or temporary state while preserving required runtime tags and eager activation of the full declared dependency tree.
steering_decision.dkr_2_input: Test the smallest generic ExecutionContext cancellation and descendant-settlement contract; do not assume spawn, start, or a task pool.
steering_decision.dkr_3_input: Define a safe extension-visible observation projection over the same context hierarchy while retaining domain SessionEvent records.
steering_decision.dkr_4_input: Express bounded queue consumers with controller(flow), awaited exec calls, and per-exec tags before proposing a new concurrency primitive.
steering_decision.dkr_5_input: Specify evidence, independent verification, freshness, authority, and idempotency required before GitHub publication.
